name: Bay Scallop Saute
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Finely shredded fresh basil leaves
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, heat the olive oil with the chopped garlic over moderate-to-high heat.
2. When the garlic sizzles, add the bay scallops and sauté just until they firm up slightly, about 30 seconds.
3. Add the diced red, green, and yellow bell peppers to the skillet and sauté for about 1 minute more.
4. Stir in the fish stock. In a small cup or bowl, whisk together the cornstarch and white wine until the mixture is smooth.
5. Gradually add the cornstarch-wine mixture into the skillet, stirring constantly.
6. Simmer the sauce until it begins to thicken slightly, about 1 minute.
7. Stir in the shredded basil leaves and toasted pine nuts.
8. Spoon the saute over the cooked pasta-thin strands.
9. Season with freshly ground black pepper to taste.
